title: hedera helix subsp. maroccana
intro: Hedera helix subsp. maroccana, also known as Moroccan ivy or Algerian ivy, is a subspecies of the common ivy plant that is native to the Atlas Mountains of Morocco. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, derived from its saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Hedera helix subsp. maroccana include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Hedera helix subsp. maroccana can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed internally to harness its medicinal properties.
